records.description: TCP port already in use — set PORT=0 to let the OS assign a free port

shortest_verification.command: node -e "const net=require('net'); const s1=net.createServer(); s1.listen(0,()=>{const port=s1.address().port; const s2=net.createServer(); s2.on('error',e=>{console.error(e.message);s1.close();process.exit(1)}); s2.listen(port)})" 2>&1 || true

fix_path.fix_command: node -e "const net=require('net'); const s=net.createServer(); s.listen(0,()=>{console.log('listening on port '+s.address().port); s.close()})" 2>&1 || true
fix_path.description: TCP port already in use — set PORT=0 to let the OS assign a free port

reproduction.fix_command: node -e "const net=require('net'); const s=net.createServer(); s.listen(0,()=>{console.log('listening on port '+s.address().port); s.close()})" 2>&1 || true

authored_fixture.fix_rationale: Changing the configured port to 0 lets the OS assign each listener a free TCP port instead of forcing both servers onto the same fixed port.
